mechanism_narrative: Declines in the federal funds rate reduce corporate borrowing costs and compress equity discount rates, generating positive forward returns for the S&P 500 over a one-month horizon.
failure_condition: Rank IC falls below 0.02 over rolling 3-year windows, or forward returns show no statistical significance (p > 0.10) during rate cut periods.

mechanism_narrative: A sharp month-over-month rise in the unemployment rate signals sudden labor market deterioration, triggering immediate risk-off positioning and negative S&P 500 returns over the next five trading days.
candidate_trigger: UNRATE increases by more than 0.2 percentage points month-over-month
failure_condition: Positive 5-day returns occur in over 55% of instances where the unemployment threshold is breached, or the signal loses predictive power when UNRATE changes are driven by seasonal adjustments.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous expansion in real personal consumption expenditures and housing starts indicates broad-based consumer demand strength, which translates into sustained corporate earnings growth and positive quarterly S&P 500 returns.
failure_condition: The joint signal fails to outperform a single-series baseline, or forward returns turn negative when consumption growth outpaces housing construction by more than two standard deviations.

mechanism_narrative: Rising industrial production indices reflect strengthening manufacturing capacity utilization, which drives positive equity returns over a one-month horizon specifically during periods of stable inflation.
failure_condition: Signal direction reverses or becomes statistically insignificant when CPIAUCSL exceeds 3.0% year-over-year, or when industrial production gains are offset by inventory drawdowns.

mechanism_narrative: Consistent month-over-month growth in total nonfarm payrolls provides a reliable leading indicator of economic resilience, generating positive short-term S&P 500 returns as markets price in continued wage growth and consumption.
failure_condition: The payroll growth signal yields negative forward returns in over 60% of observations, or loses predictive accuracy when revisions to prior months exceed 50,000 jobs.

mechanism_narrative: When consumer price inflation breaches the 2.5% annual threshold, markets anticipate aggressive central bank tightening, which compresses valuation multiples and produces negative quarterly S&P 500 returns.
failure_condition: Equity markets rally despite high inflation readings, or the negative return pattern disappears when inflation spikes are driven exclusively by volatile energy components.

mechanism_narrative: A divergence where industrial production declines while real GDP growth remains positive indicates supply-side bottlenecks and margin compression, leading to negative one-month equity returns as earnings forecasts are downgraded.
failure_condition: The divergence signal fails to predict negative returns when GDP growth is driven by government spending rather than private investment, or when inventory adjustments mask true production trends.

mechanism_narrative: Accelerating real personal consumption expenditures signal robust household balance sheets, which drives immediate positive market momentum over a one-week horizon during periods of accommodative monetary policy.
failure_condition: Consumption acceleration coincides with rising household debt service ratios, causing the signal to flip negative, or forward returns show no statistical edge when FEDFUNDS remains above 4.0%.
